root_text.an3.27:1.3: Atthi, bhikkhave, puggalo jigucchitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:1.4: Atthi, bhikkhave, puggalo ajjhupekkhitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:1.5: Atthi, bhikkhave, puggalo sevitabbo bhajitabbo payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:1.6: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo jigucchitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo?
root_text.an3.27:1.7: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dussīlo hoti pāpadhammo asuci saṅkassarasamācāro paṭicchannakammanto, assamaṇo samaṇapaṭiñño, abrahmacārī brahmacāripaṭiñño, antopūti avassuto kasambujāto.
root_text.an3.27:1.8: Evarūpo, bhikkhave, puggalo jigucchitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:1.10: Kiñcāpi, bhikkhave, evarūpassa puggalassa na diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati, atha kho naṁ pāpako kittisaddo abbhuggacchati:
root_text.an3.27:1.12: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ahi gūthagato kiñcāpi na daṁsati, atha kho naṁ makkheti;
root_text.an3.27:1.13: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, kiñcāpi evarūpassa puggalassa na diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati, atha kho naṁ pāpako kittisaddo abbhuggacchati:
root_text.an3.27:1.15: Tasmā evarūpo puggalo jigucchitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:2.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo ajjhupekkhitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo?
root_text.an3.27:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo kodhano hoti upāyāsabahulo,
root_text.an3.27:2.3: appampi vutto samāno abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an3.27:2.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, duṭṭhāruko kaṭṭhena vā kaṭhalāya vā ghaṭṭito bhiyyoso mattāya āsavaṁ deti;
root_text.an3.27:2.6: seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, tindukālātaṁ kaṭṭhena vā kaṭhalāya vā ghaṭṭitaṁ bhiyyoso mattāya cicciṭāyati ciṭiciṭāyati;
root_text.an3.27:2.8: seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gūthakūpo kaṭṭhena vā kaṭhalāya vā ghaṭṭito bhiyyoso mattāya duggandho hoti;
root_text.an3.27:2.9: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo kodhano hoti upāyāsabahulo,
root_text.an3.27:2.10: appampi vutto samāno abhisajjati kuppati byāpajjati patitthīyati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti.
root_text.an3.27:2.11: Evarūpo, bhikkhave, puggalo ajjhupekkhitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:2.13: Akkoseyyapi maṁ paribhāseyyapi maṁ anatthampi maṁ kareyyāti.
root_text.an3.27:2.14: Tasmā evarūpo puggalo ajjhupekkhitabbo na sevitabbo na bhajitabbo na payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:3.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, puggalo sevitabbo bhajitabbo payirupāsitabbo?
root_text.an3.27:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sīlavā hoti kalyāṇadhammo.
root_text.an3.27:3.3: Evarūpo, bhikkhave, puggalo sevitabbo bhajitabbo payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:3.5: Kiñcāpi, bhikkhave, evarūpassa puggalassa na diṭṭhānugatiṁ āpajjati, atha kho naṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchati:
root_text.an3.27:3.7: Tasmā evarūpo puggalo sevitabbo bhajitabbo payirupāsitabbo.
root_text.an3.27:3.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasminti.

translation_text.an3.27:1.1: “These three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.27:1.3: There is an individual you should be disgusted by, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:1.4: There is an individual you should regard with equanimity, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:1.5: There is an individual you should associate with, accompany, and attend.
translation_text.an3.27:1.6: Who is the individual you should be disgusted by, and not associate with, accompany, or attend?
translation_text.an3.27:1.7: It’s an individual who is unethical, of bad qualities, filthy, with suspicious behavior, underhand, no true ascetic or spiritual practitioner—though claiming to be one—rotten inside, festering, and depraved.
translation_text.an3.27:1.8: You should be disgusted by such an individual, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:1.10: Even if you don’t follow the example of such an individual, you still get a bad reputation:
translation_text.an3.27:1.11: ‘That individual has bad friends, companions, and associates.’
translation_text.an3.27:1.12: They’re like a snake that’s been living in a pile of dung. Even if it doesn’t bite, it’ll still rub off on you.
translation_text.an3.27:1.13: In the same way, even if you don’t follow the example of such an individual, you still get a bad reputation:
translation_text.an3.27:1.14: ‘That individual has bad friends, companions, and associates.’
translation_text.an3.27:1.15: That’s why you should be disgusted by such an individual, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:2.1: Who is the individual you should regard with equanimity, and not associate with, accompany, or attend?
translation_text.an3.27:2.2: It’s an individual who is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.27:2.3: Even when lightly criticized they lose their temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and they display annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an3.27:2.4: They’re like a festering sore, which, when you hit it with a stick or a stone, discharges even more.
translation_text.an3.27:2.5: In the same way, someone is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.27:2.6: They’re like a firebrand of pale-moon ebony, which, when you hit it with a stick or a stone, sizzles and crackles even more.
translation_text.an3.27:2.7: In the same way, someone is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.27:2.8: They’re like a sewer, which, when you stir it with a stick or a stone, stinks even more.
translation_text.an3.27:2.9: In the same way, an individual is irritable and bad-tempered.
translation_text.an3.27:2.10: Even when lightly criticized they lose their temper, becoming annoyed, hostile, and hard-hearted, and they display annoyance, hate, and bitterness.
translation_text.an3.27:2.11: You should regard such an individual with equanimity, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:2.13: Thinking, ‘They might abuse or insult me, or do me harm.’
translation_text.an3.27:2.14: That’s why you should regard such an individual with equanimity, and you shouldn’t associate with, accompany, or attend them.
translation_text.an3.27:3.1: Who is the individual you should associate with, accompany, and attend?
translation_text.an3.27:3.2: It’s an individual who is ethical, of good character.
translation_text.an3.27:3.3: You should associate with, accompany, and attend such an individual.
translation_text.an3.27:3.5: Even if you don’t follow the example of such an individual, you still get a good reputation:
translation_text.an3.27:3.6: ‘That individual has good friends, companions, and associates.’
translation_text.an3.27:3.7: That’s why you should associate with, accompany, and attend such an individual.
translation_text.an3.27:3.8: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.27:4.1: A man who associates with an inferior goes downhill,
translation_text.an3.27:4.2: but associating with an equal, you’ll never decline;
translation_text.an3.27:4.3: following the best, you’ll quickly rise up,
translation_text.an3.27:4.4: so you should keep company <j>with people better than you.”

variant_text.an3.27:2.4: duṭṭhāruko → duṭṭhārukā (bj) | kaṭhalāya → kaṭhalena (bj) | ghaṭṭito → ghaṭṭitā (bj) | āsavaṁ deti → assandati (bj)
